task_completion.description: Open the Webots application and load the initial simulation world.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Webots GUI is open and displays a 3D soccer field environment with multiple robots.

task_completion.description: Locate the robot nodes within the Webots scene tree.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The scene tree panel (usually on the left side of the Webots interface) is expanded, showing robot nodes such as 'B1 Robot' or similar.

task_completion.description: Inspect the controller field of the robots to identify the configuration issue.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The properties of a robot node are expanded in the scene tree or field editor, specifically showing the 'controller' field with the incorrect value 'soccer_player_broken'.

task_completion.description: Change the broken controllers to the correct soccer demo controller.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'controller' field for the robots is actively being edited or is shown to be successfully changed to 'soccer_player' in the Webots interface or a text editor modifying the .wbt file.

task_completion.description: Save the modified world to the specified output path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save World As...' dialog box showing the path '/home/ga/Desktop/swarm_ready.wbt', a terminal command saving the file to this path, or the Webots window title updating to 'swarm_ready.wbt'.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent actually modified the world file rather than creating a blank or dummy file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent interacting with the Webots GUI or a text editor containing the actual Webots world structure, not just using 'touch' or echoing text into the target file.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent fixed the controller field and didn't just rename the robot nodes.
integrity.visual_evidence: The modification must specifically target the 'controller' field (changing it to 'soccer_player'), leaving the robot's DEF or name fields intact unless explicitly required.

integrity.description: Verify the agent executed the save operation and didn't just type the path without confirming.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the confirmation of the save action, such as the file appearing on the Desktop, the Webots window title changing, or a successful terminal command execution.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The base world is a Webots soccer demo (soccer.wbt). There are 6 robots in the scene (named 'B1 Robot', etc.) that have their controllers incorrectly set to 'soccer_player_broken'. The agent must change these controllers to 'soccer_player' and save the corrected world exactly to '/home/ga/Desktop/swarm_ready.wbt'. The programmatic verifier for this task is known to be broken regarding position checks, so rely strictly on visual evidence of the controller fixes and the file save operation.
